name: Spiced Lentils with Apple Crisps and Curried
recipeIngredient: 1 large Yellow onion, peeled and finely diced
1 tsp Garam masala (Spice mixture sold at Indian food stores or other specialty shops)
2 Granny Smith apples, peeled, cored, and finely diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to its lowest setting.
2. In a small bowl, dissolve 1 tbsp confectioner's sugar in the juice of 2 lemons.
3. Thinly slice 2 Granny Smith apples horizontally into rounds approximately 1/16-inch thick.
4. Lay the apple slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush lightly with the sugared lemon juice.
5. Place the baking sheet in the oven and allow the apples to dry, about 3 to 4 hours or overnight.
6. Remove the baking sheet from the oven and gently peel the apple slices from the paper. (Some discoloration of the apple slices will occur.)
7. Store the dried apple slices in an airtight container until ready to use. They will last for 2 days in the refrigerator.
8. In a small non-stick skillet, heat over medium heat.
9. Add 1 tsp curry powder and toast until you can smell its intense aroma, about 30 seconds.
10. Remove pan from heat.
11. Combine the toasted curry powder with 2 bananas that have been peeled and finely diced, and 2 cups plain yogurt in a serving bowl.
12. Stir well and refrigerate until ready to use.
13. In a large, heavy saucepan, heat the oil over medium-high heat.
14. Add the onion and saute until soft, about 4 minutes.
15. Add the garam masala, bay leaf, and apples, and cook, stirring continuously, for 2 more minutes.
16. Add the lentils and stock and bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
17. Reduce to a simmer and cook for about 25 minutes or until the lentils are tender, being careful not to overcook them.
18. Remove the pan from the heat, remove the bay leaf, and adjust seasoning to taste.
19. Serve the lentils hot in one large serving bowl.
20. Serve the yogurt and apple crisps separately and invite diners to combine all three components to their personal taste. Makes 4 appetizer or 2 main course servings.
